journal_entry.reason: Under IFRS, when preferred shares are converted to common shares, the carrying value of the preferred shares is transferred to the common shares at book value. No gain or loss is recognized on this equity-to-equity conversion. The 50 preferred shares with a carrying value of $100 are removed from Preferred Shares (debit to decrease equity), and 25 common shares are issued with the same $100 value (credit to increase equity).

output_debit_classifier.reason: This is a share conversion transaction where 50 preferred shares with a carrying value of $100 are converted into 25 common shares. Under IFRS, equity conversions are recorded at book value with no gain or loss recognized. The carrying value is simply transferred from one equity account (Preferred Shares) to another (Issued Capital for common shares). The transaction involves one debit to decrease the Preferred Shares equity account and one credit to increase the common shares equity account (Issued Capital), both for $100. No tax applies to equity conversions.
